generated_for: IDBI Innovate 2026 · Track 03 · concept build (synthetic data)

validation.note: Synthetic-population validation of the architecture — not a real-world performance claim. Re-estimated on IDBI sandbox data post-shortlist.

L.label: Liquidity
L.question: Can the business absorb a shock this month?

S.label: Stability
S.question: How steady is the cash engine?

G.label: Growth
G.question: Which direction is the business moving?

C.label: Compliance
C.question: Is the regulatory house in order?

K.label: Concentration
K.question: How fragile is the revenue base?

msmes.name: Chandra Kirana Stores
msmes.story: New-to-credit neighbourhood store. No audited financials, no bureau file — but a vivid daily UPI pulse and six years of steady trade.

positive.text: EPFO deposit regularity of 100%; 22 consecutive months of on-time GST filing
refund/reversal rate of 0.9%; top-3 payers at 13% of revenue
seasonal swing of 53% around mean revenue; turnover volatility of 14%
worst-decile balance at 5% of monthly outflow; 0 end-of-day negative balance days
0 EMI/NACH bounce(s) in 12 months; existing obligations at 8.7% of turnover

negative.text: new-customer acquisition at 13.8%/month; payroll wage growth of +5.9%

what_if.action: File GSTR-3B on time for the next 3 months
Hold a 30-day cash buffer in the current account

guards: Liquidity floor: buffer < 15 days — limit reduced 20%
covenants: Maintain minimum average balance of 15 days' outflow
File GSTR-3B on or before due date throughout the loan tenor
Route >= 60% of business receipts through the IDBI current account
early_warning: End-of-day negative balance on > 5 days in any month

msmes.memo:
## Underwriting Memo — Chandra Kirana Stores
*Retail Trade · Indore, MP · 6 yrs vintage · Data tier T1 · Proprietor: Ramesh Chandra*

**NAADI Score: 762 ± 40 (Grade A) · PD(12m) 1.4% · Fast-track approve**

### Recommendation: Approve
Working-capital line of **₹920,000** over **36 months** at an indicative 11.0% p.a. Serviceable EMI ₹37,743 against a verified monthly surplus of ₹75,486.

### Why (evidence-linked)
- **+88.2 pts · Compliance** — EPFO deposit regularity of 100%; 22 consecutive months of on-time GST filing `[RC-C05]`
- **+25.9 pts · Concentration** — refund/reversal rate of 0.9%; top-3 payers at 13% of revenue `[RC-K06]`
- **+20.9 pts · Stability** — seasonal swing of 53% around mean revenue; turnover volatility of 14% `[RC-S02]`
- **-7.7 pts · Growth** — new-customer acquisition at 13.8%/month; payroll wage growth of +5.9% `[RC-G03]`

### Policy guards applied
- Liquidity floor: buffer < 15 days — limit reduced 20%

### Covenants
- Maintain minimum average balance of 15 days' outflow
- File GSTR-3B on or before due date throughout the loan tenor
- Route >= 60% of business receipts through the IDBI current account

### Early-warning triggers
- 3-month turnover drawdown > 25% vs trailing average
- Two consecutive delayed GSTR-3B filings
- End-of-day negative balance on > 5 days in any month

*Prepared by Munshi. Every figure traces to a consented data rail; final authority rests with the sanctioning officer.*

msmes.name: Meenakshi Textiles
msmes.story: Established exporter with strong compliance — but over half of revenue rides on a single overseas buyer.

positive.text: EPFO deposit regularity of 88%; worst GSTR-3B filing delay of 2 days
payroll wage growth of +8.4%; 12-month turnover trend of +6.2%/month
0 end-of-day negative balance days; cash buffer of 3 days of outflow
0 EMI/NACH bounce(s) in 12 months; existing obligations at 5.6% of turnover

negative.text: seasonal swing of 108% around mean revenue; turnover volatility of 28%
refund/reversal rate of 0.3%; payer concentration (HHI) of 0.34

guards: Liquidity floor: buffer < 15 days — limit reduced 20%
Concentration cap: top payer > 50% — limit reduced 25%
covenants: Maintain minimum average balance of 15 days' outflow
Diversify: no single payer above 50% of receipts by month 12
File GSTR-3B on or before due date throughout the loan tenor
Route >= 60% of business receipts through the IDBI current account
early_warning: End-of-day negative balance on > 5 days in any month

msmes.memo:
## Underwriting Memo — Meenakshi Textiles
*Textiles · Surat, GJ · 11 yrs vintage · Data tier T3 · Proprietor: Meenakshi Patel*

**NAADI Score: 711 ± 15 (Grade B) · PD(12m) 2.0% · Approve with standard covenants**

### Recommendation: Approve
Working-capital line of **₹18,870,000** over **24 months** at an indicative 12.0% p.a. Serviceable EMI ₹1,480,370 against a verified monthly surplus of ₹3,289,711.

### Why (evidence-linked)
- **+53.8 pts · Compliance** — EPFO deposit regularity of 88%; worst GSTR-3B filing delay of 2 days `[RC-C05]`
- **+36.4 pts · Growth** — payroll wage growth of +8.4%; 12-month turnover trend of +6.2%/month `[RC-G03]`
- **+9.9 pts · Liquidity** — 0 end-of-day negative balance days; cash buffer of 3 days of outflow `[RC-L01]`
- **-8.4 pts · Stability** — seasonal swing of 108% around mean revenue; turnover volatility of 28% `[RC-S02]`
- **-8.4 pts · Concentration** — refund/reversal rate of 0.3%; payer concentration (HHI) of 0.34 `[RC-K06]`

### Policy guards applied
- Liquidity floor: buffer < 15 days — limit reduced 20%
- Concentration cap: top payer > 50% — limit reduced 25%

### Covenants
- Maintain minimum average balance of 15 days' outflow
- Diversify: no single payer above 50% of receipts by month 12
- File GSTR-3B on or before due date throughout the loan tenor
- Route >= 60% of business receipts through the IDBI current account

### Early-warning triggers
- 3-month turnover drawdown > 25% vs trailing average
- Two consecutive delayed GSTR-3B filings
- End-of-day negative balance on > 5 days in any month

*Prepared by Munshi. Every figure traces to a consented data rail; final authority rests with the sanctioning officer.*

## Underwriting Memo — Ganpati Constructions
*Construction · Ahmedabad, GJ · 12 yrs vintage · Data tier T2 · Proprietor: Jignesh Trivedi*

**NAADI Score: 543 ± 25 (Grade D) · PD(12m) 21.9% · Small-ticket starter products**

### Recommendation: Offer starter product

### Why (evidence-linked)
- **-26.9 pts · Stability** — seasonal swing of 226% around mean revenue; turnover volatility of 84% `[RC-S02]`
- **-25.7 pts · Compliance** — EPFO deposit regularity of 74%; average GSTR-3B filing delay of 0.6 days `[RC-C05]`
- **-13.8 pts · Liquidity** — worst-decile balance at 0% of monthly outflow; cash buffer of 1 days of outflow `[RC-L01]`

### Policy guards applied
- Liquidity floor: buffer < 15 days — limit reduced 20%

### Covenants
- Maintain minimum average balance of 15 days' outflow
- File GSTR-3B on or before due date throughout the loan tenor
- Zero EMI/NACH bounces; auto-review on first bounce
- Route >= 60% of business receipts through the IDBI current account

### Early-warning triggers
- 3-month turnover drawdown > 25% vs trailing average
- Two consecutive delayed GSTR-3B filings
- End-of-day negative balance on > 5 days in any month

*Prepared by Munshi. Every figure traces to a consented data rail; final authority rests with the sanctioning officer.*
